column1: # SailFrames E1 — custom OTA-capable partition table (4MB flash).
# Replaces the core's `no_ota` scheme (single 2MB app + 2MB unused SPIFFS)
# with a dual-app A/B layout so a future OTA update can flash the inactive
# slot and boot-swap. SPIFFS is dropped entirely: this firmware stores
# everything on the SD card (storage.cpp) and never touches SPIFFS/LittleFS.
# OTA itself is NOT yet implemented — this table is the one piece OTA cannot
# retrofit onto itself (an already-flashed device can't repartition over the
# air)
column2: so it is committed now to avoid a fleet-wide USB reflash later. See

column1: # docs/ota.md for the full rationale and the intended future OTA flow.
# Offsets line up with .github/workflows/firmware-release.yml's merge step
# which is why that step is unchanged: app0 at 0x10000 (the app image) and
# otadata at 0xe000 (boot_app0.bin — the "boot app0" selector). Selecting
# this table needs PartitionScheme=custom in the build FQBN (arduino-cli /
# Arduino IDE then use this sketch-local partitions.csv).
# Two ~1.94MB app slots vs. the ~1.6MB image → ~340KB headroom per slot.
# Name
nvs
otadata
app0
app1
